title: achillea formosa subsp. amanica
intro: Achillea formosa subsp. amanica, also known as the 'Yarrow Plant', is a perennial herb native to China, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for wound healing, digestive issues, and relieving menstrual cramps. Achillea formosa subsp. amanica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used topically to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever.
